title: ICS Protocol Violation Alerts
spl:
index=ot sourcetype="ics:protocol"
| where severity IN ("high","critical") OR match(message,"(?i)(malformed|illegal|out.of.range)")
| stats count by protocol, src, dest, signature
| sort -count
description: Malformed DNP3/Modbus/Profinet frames or wrong L4 ports indicate misconfiguration or attacks.
value: Malformed DNP3/Modbus/Profinet frames or wrong L4 ports indicate misconfiguration or attacks.
implementation: Normalize IDS fields into Splunk. Tune for false positives on legacy equipment. Route critical to SOC and OT jointly.
knownFalsePositives: Protocol noise or delays during path switches, field device resets, or winter RF conditions on radio links.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Industrial IDS, Zeek ICS parsers.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="ics:protocol"` IDS alerts.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Normalize IDS fields into Splunk. Tune for false positives on legacy equipment. Route critical to SOC and OT jointly.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="ics:protocol"
| where severity IN ("high","critical") OR match(message,"(?i)(malformed|illegal|out.of.range)")
| stats count by protocol, src, dest, signature
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**ICS Protocol Violation Alerts** — Malformed DNP3/Modbus/Profinet frames or wrong L4 ports indicate misconfiguration or attacks.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="ics:protocol"` IDS alerts. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Industrial IDS, Zeek ICS parsers. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot; **sourcetype**: ics:protocol. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot, sourcetype="ics:protocol". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where severity IN ("high","critical") OR match(message,"(?i)(malformed|illegal|out.of.range)")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by protocol, src, dest, signature** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search when the process is in a known state. Compare key fields and alarm states to the SCADA or DCS HMI and, where relevant, the engineering tool for the PLC. Confirm sourcetypes and field extractions match the vendor feed.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (violations), Timeline (events), Sankey (src → dest).
grandmaExplanation: We watch your plant control systems and field data. We help you know if something is off with this area: ics protocol violation alerts before harm, damage, or wasted effort piles up.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable (no CIM or Operational_Telemetry data model in this SPL).
